title: Killhouse delegation record
description: One JSONL record per subagent delegation, captured at the delegation boundary. Pre-delegation fields are frozen before the subagent runs; `outcome` is finalized after it returns. The record must carry enough to replay the delegation in isolation (Gate 0: HERMETIC requires pinning repo SHA and the gate's cwd).

depends_on.description: delegation_ids this delegation consumed. May be empty, but the field must be present.

resolved_prompt.description: Verbatim prompt sent to the subagent, frozen before it runs.

chosen_model.description: Actual runtime model id used for this delegation, when the runtime exposes it. chosen_tier remains the actual executed tier.

tier_price.description: Price of the chosen tier, not just its identity. `basis` records whether the input/output numbers are real (configured, from a prices map), a documented stand-in (illustrative), or unknown (unpriced). Never invent numbers: under current-model-only routing where no price exists, use basis 'unpriced' and omit them.

decision_signals.description: The classify/triage output plus the confidence/reasoning that drove the tier, captured BEFORE the subagent runs.

routing_request.description: Optional future router-serving request metadata, frozen at the delegation boundary before delegated output exists. This is the client-side request contract Killhouse sends to a router, not a route-logic training label.

signals_snapshot.description: Request features derived from the delegation boundary. Must not depend on subagent output.

router_decision.description: Optional future router-serving response consumed by Killhouse. selected_tier/model are the router recommendation; chosen_tier/chosen_model record what Killhouse actually executed. The recommendation is advisory until Killhouse runs the logged gate and finalizes outcome.

applied.description: True only when Killhouse executed the router-selected tier. If false, fallback_reason explains why chosen_tier differs.

gate.description: The exact verification the delegation's output is judged by. cwd is required: Gate 0 proved replay fidelity depends on it.

upstream_artifacts.description: Resolved inputs consumed, enough to replay. Must include a repository_state pinning the VCS head.

dirty_files.description: Dirty files at freeze time. Empty means the worktree was clean; non-empty entries must include enough path/status and pinned content data for replay.

outcome.description: Finalized after the subagent returns. A fired escalation is a ground-truth 'guessed-too-low' label: record its magnitude and trigger, not just the bit.

escalation_magnitude.description: Tiers jumped when escalation fired. Required when escalated is true.

escalation_trigger.description: The signal that triggered escalation. Required when escalated is true.
